markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Verify isolated chunks do not create off-topic bridge edges or misleading graph retrieval. This MT belongs to SecurityPortabilityValidation. Prove WP-009 behavior with negative fixtures, visual debugging, portability checks, and validator-ready evidence.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Validation must include negative fixtures for false PASS, stale state, missing Postgres, missing assets, secret leakage, partial PDF extraction, parallel write conflicts, and UI overlap.
Portability checks must reject hardcoded machine paths and authority stored only in generated projections.
scope.expected_behavior: FragmentedGraphFixture leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: FragmentedGraphFixture is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence.summary: MT-232 now prevents fragmented graph retrieval from using misleading bridge evidence. Schema-first filtering carries ontology term maps_to_edge_type into executed graph traversal; schema and caller edge policies intersect, with an explicit deny_all_edges state for disjoint policies so empty intersections cannot widen. GraphTraversalPlanner now expands only active knowledge_edges, so proposed and retired bridge suggestions cannot become graph candidates, ranked evidence, or persisted bundle items. HBR-STOP-001/HBR-STOP-002 and CX-503R are proven by real PostgreSQL negative fixtures; HBR-VIS-005 is not applicable because this MT changes no GUI surface.

commands.result: RED before schema-edge policy hardening with graph_edge_count left 2 right 1; RED again after reviewer finding for disjoint schema/caller allowlists with graph_edge_count left 2 right 0; GREEN after edge_type_allowlist propagation plus explicit deny_all_edges intersection handling

RED before active-edge traversal filtering with proposed bridge graph_edge_count left 1 right 0; GREEN after GraphTraversalPlanner filters active knowledge_edges before degree calculation and expansion
PASS; 15/15 retrieval pipeline tests including MT-232 off-topic bridge, disjoint policy, proposed bridge, retired bridge, graph, fallback, trace, ranking, and bundle persistence paths
PASS; 3/3 schema-first, ProjectBrain, and ContextPack bridge tests
PASS earlier in the MT-232 run; 2/2 bridge generator tests. A later rerun after the reviewer fix timed out at 5 minutes and is not claimed as passing; scoped no-run compile below passed afterward.

commands.command: cargo test --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml --features test-utils --test knowledge_retrieval_pipeline_tests --test knowledge_retrieval_bridges_tests --test knowledge_memory_bridge_tests --no-run
commands.result: PASS; affected integration targets compile after reviewer fix and doc correction
Product commit succeeded; hook did not auto-send review request because commit subject was not an MT auto-relay subject. Explicit adversarial review was run in-session and returned PASS.

evidence.summary: Reviewer initially found a blocker: schema/caller disjoint edge allowlists collapsed to empty and widened traversal because empty GraphTraversalPolicy allowlist meant unrestricted. The blocker was hardened with an explicit deny_all_edges state plus a real PostgreSQL disjoint-policy regression. Re-review found no blocking issues; the only non-blocking note was stale module-header wording about entity_edge_degree, corrected before product commit.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
